specification.topic-statement: The toolset filter restricts which tools from the tool registry are offered to the LLM on each request, allowing an agent to work with a named subset of available tools.

scope.in-scope: The whitelist data structure on the agent, the /toolset command (setting, listing, and clearing the filter), delimiter parsing, and database persistence. Toolset inheritance during agent forking is covered in the agent fork specification.
scope.boundary: How the whitelist is applied when building the tools array for an LLM request (filtering the tool registry) is covered by the LLM request specification.
How tools are discovered and registered is covered by the tool execution specification.

behavior.whitelist-storage: The toolset filter is stored on the agent as a dynamically sized array of tool name strings, alongside a count of entries. An empty array (count of zero) means no filter is active and all registered tools are offered to the LLM. A non-empty array means only tools whose names appear in the list are offered.
behavior.toolset-command:
The `/toolset` command with a tool name list sets the filter, replacing any existing filter entirely:
1. The argument string is tokenized using both spaces and commas as delimiters (they may be mixed freely: `"tool1, tool2 tool3"` is valid). 2. Each token becomes one entry in the new whitelist array. 3. The existing whitelist is replaced. 4. A toolset event is persisted to the database with the original argument string (not the tokenized form).
The `/toolset` command with no arguments lists the current whitelist and takes no other action.
Users may separate tool names with spaces, commas, or a mix of both. The delimiter style is not preserved — on session restoration, the original argument string is replayed and re-tokenized.
behavior.clear-behavior: Providing an argument string that contains no non-whitespace tokens (for example, an argument containing only spaces or commas) results in a zero-count filter, which is equivalent to no filter (all tools visible). No explicit "clear" sub-command exists; providing an empty effective argument achieves this effect.
behavior.persistence: Toolset changes are persisted to the database as command events containing the original argument string. During session restoration, the most recent toolset command in the agent's history is replayed to reconstruct the filter. If no toolset command exists in the history but the agent was forked, the toolset is inherited from the fork event.
